title: rosa canina var. euoxyphlla
intro: Rosa canina var. euoxyphlla, also known as the dog rose or wild rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina species that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rosa canina var. euoxyphlla include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
